2782	\section{Absolute sheaf cohomology as Ext of the structure sheaf}
2783	
2784	The two vanishing lemmas below speak about the \emph{absolute} sheaf cohomology
2785	\(H^p(U, \mathcal{F})\) of an \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module over an open \(U\). Their
2786	dimension-shift proof needs three structural facts about \(H^p(U, -)\): a degree-zero
2787	identification with global sections, vanishing on injective modules, and a long exact
2788	sequence attached to a short exact sequence of modules. We realize \(H^p(U, -)\) as the
2789	Ext of the structure sheaf, where Mathlib supplies all three facts off the shelf; the
2790	following definition fixes that realization, and the Mathlib dependency anchors after it
2791	pin the exact results it rests on.
2792	
2793	\begin{definition}
2794	[Absolute sheaf cohomology as Ext of the structure sheaf]
2795	  \label{def:absolute_cohomology}
2796	  \uses{lem:ext_bifunctor_mathlib, lem:ext_covariant_les_mathlib,
2797	    lem:ext_eq_zero_of_injective_mathlib, lem:ext_homequiv_zero_mathlib,
2798	    lem:hasext_standard_mathlib, lem:modules_restrict_functor_mathlib}
2799	  \textit{Realization of absolute sheaf cohomology in the module-sheaf category.}
2800	  Let \(X\) be a scheme, \(\mathcal{F}\) an \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, and \(U \subseteq
2801	  X\) an open subscheme with open immersion \(j : U \hookrightarrow X\). Write
2802	  \(\mathcal{F}|_U\) for the restriction of \(\mathcal{F}\) along \(j\) — the image of
2803	  \(\mathcal{F}\) under the Mathlib restriction functor \(j^\ast :
2804	  X.\mathrm{Modules} \to U.\mathrm{Modules}\) of
2805	  Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_restrict_functor_mathlib} — and \(\mathcal{O}_U\) for the
2806	  structure sheaf of \(U\) regarded as a module over itself. The \emph{absolute sheaf
2807	  cohomology of \(\mathcal{F}\) over \(U\)} is defined to be the Ext of the structure
2808	  sheaf in the abelian category \(U.\mathrm{Modules} = \mathrm{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_U)\) of
2809	  sheaves of \(\mathcal{O}_U\)-modules:
2810	  \[
2811	    H^p(U, \mathcal{F}) \;:=\; \operatorname{Ext}^p_{\mathrm{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_U)}
2812	      \bigl(\mathcal{O}_U,\; \mathcal{F}|_U\bigr),
2813	    \qquad p \geq 0,
2814	  \]
2815	  equivalently \(\operatorname{Ext}^p_{\mathrm{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_X)}(j_!\mathcal{O}_U,
2816	  \mathcal{F})\). Here \(\operatorname{Ext}\) is Mathlib's Ext bifunctor
2817	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:ext_bifunctor_mathlib}) on the abelian category
2818	  \(U.\mathrm{Modules}\); the \(\mathrm{HasExt}\) structure it requires is available
2819	  unconditionally by Lemma~\ref{lem:hasext_standard_mathlib}. The three structural facts
2820	  the downstream dimension-shift arguments consume are then exactly the corresponding
2821	  Mathlib results:
2822	  \begin{enumerate}
2823	  \item \textbf{Degree zero is global sections.} In degree \(0\), Ext is the Hom-group,
2824	    \(\operatorname{Ext}^0(\mathcal{O}_U, \mathcal{F}|_U) \cong
2825	    \operatorname{Hom}_{\mathcal{O}_U}(\mathcal{O}_U, \mathcal{F}|_U) = \Gamma(U,
2826	    \mathcal{F})\), by the degree-zero Ext comparison
2827	    (Lemma~\ref{lem:ext_homequiv_zero_mathlib}); so \(H^0(U, \mathcal{F}) \cong
2828	    \Gamma(U, \mathcal{F})\).
2829	  \item \textbf{Injective vanishing.} When the \emph{second} Ext argument is an
2830	    injective module \(\mathcal{J}\), one has \(\operatorname{Ext}^{n+1}(-,
2831	    \mathcal{J}) = 0\) by Lemma~\ref{lem:ext_eq_zero_of_injective_mathlib}. Placing an
2832	    injective \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module \(\mathcal{I}\) in that second-argument role
2833	    (via the \(j_!\mathcal{O}_U\) form over \(\mathrm{Mod}(\mathcal{O}_X)\)) yields
2834	    \(H^{n+1}(U, \mathcal{I}) = 0\) directly, with no appeal to restriction preserving
2835	    injectives.
2836	  \item \textbf{Long exact sequence.} For a short exact sequence \(0 \to \mathcal{F}
2837	    \to \mathcal{I} \to \mathcal{Q} \to 0\) of modules and the \emph{fixed} first
2838	    argument \(\mathcal{O}_U\), the covariant Ext long exact sequence
2839	    (Lemma~\ref{lem:ext_covariant_les_mathlib}) is the long exact sequence of the
2840	    groups \(H^p(U, -)\), with connecting maps given by composition with the Ext class
2841	    of the extension.
2842	  \end{enumerate}
2843	\end{definition}
